pairs.question: What is the current name of the stadium previously known as Feroz Shah Kotla Stadium?
pairs.answer: The stadium's current name is the Arun Jaitley Cricket Stadium, renamed in memory of former DDCA President and Finance Minister Arun Jaitley on 12 September 2019.

pairs.question: Which cricket stadium is the second-oldest functional international cricket stadium in India, and where is it located?
pairs.answer: The Arun Jaitley Cricket Stadium (formerly Feroz Shah Kotla Stadium) is the second-oldest functional international cricket stadium in India. It is located on Bahadur Shah Zafar Marg, New Delhi, on land that was historically part of Ferozabad, the 14th-century city established by Sultan Feroz Shah Tughlaq.

pairs.question: List the cricket formats and the number of matches hosted by the Arun Jaitley Cricket Stadium as of 12 February 2026.
pairs.answer: As of 12 February 2026, the Arun Jaitley Cricket Stadium has hosted 36 Tests, 29 One Day Internationals (ODIs), and 10 Twenty20 Internationals (T20Is).

pairs.question: How many years did the Indian national cricket team remain unbeaten in Test matches as of 2024?
pairs.answer: As of 2024, the Indian national cricket team had been undefeated in Test matches for over 37 years.

pairs.question: What notable change was made to the Arun Jaitley Cricket Stadium in 2019, and what controversy did it generate?
pairs.answer: In 2019, the stadium was renamed from Feroz Shah Kotla Stadium to Arun Jaitley Cricket Stadium in memory of former DDCA President and Finance Minister Arun Jaitley. The name change was criticized by former Indian captain Bishan Singh Bedi.

pairs.question: Provide the names of the stands in the Arun Jaitley Cricket Stadium dedicated to former Indian cricket players.
pairs.answer: The stands in the Arun Jaitley Cricket Stadium are named after former India captain Bishan Singh Bedi, former India all-rounder Mohinder Amarnath, former India and Delhi opener Gautam Gambhir, and former Indian captain Virat Kohli.

pairs.question: What is the historical significance of the land on which the Arun Jaitley Cricket Stadium is located?
pairs.answer: The Arun Jaitley Cricket Stadium is situated on land that was part of Ferozabad, the 14th-century city established by Sultan Feroz Shah Tughlaq in 1354 CE. It is within the fortified city and shares proximity with historical structures such as the Kotla complex and the Jami Masjid.
